message:
test(ft-conformance): polygamma scipy subprocess conformance [bd-b5of]

Adds torch_polygamma_scipy_subprocess_conformance covering 21
(order, x) pairs:
  - trigamma  (n=1) at  1, 1.5, 2, 3, 5, 7.999..., 8.0, 8.000...,
              10, 50, 100, e, π
  - tetragamma (n=2) at 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 10, 100, π
The recurrence-shift boundary at x=8 is sampled three ways
(7.999_999_999, 8.0, 8.000_000_001) so the asymptotic-vs-recurrence
seam is locked. Skips n=0 (delegates to digamma, already covered by
torch_digamma_scipy_subprocess_conformance) and skips non-positive x
(reflection-formula path is not what polygamma_approx implements).

Tolerance is ABS_TOL=5e-5 OR REL_TOL=1e-4 — looser than the digamma
conformance's 1e-9 because polygamma_approx layers a finite-difference
cascade on top of digamma_approx, and the empirical envelope at small
integer x is ~5e-5 (matches the bound polygamma_known_values uses
internally). Bumping this floor would require porting a
libm-quality polygamma; tracked under frankentorch-b5of.

Sister harness to torch_digamma_scipy_subprocess_conformance —
same scipy oracle, same fail-loud structure, same NaN/inf
bit-equivalence pattern. Adds .gitignore entry for target-local/
so an isolated CARGO_TARGET_DIR doesn't get committed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -46,6 +46,7 @@ rch-install/
 .agent-mail-project-id
 target-codex*/
 target_*/
+target-local/
 
 # Local legacy-oracle mirrors (too large for source repo)
 /legacy_*/
